justification: No handoff brief was produced. The escalation explicitly identifies Bird's missing domain analysis as the blocker, names both missing components (domain rules and acceptance criteria), and constitutes a formal stop with a clear recommendation to wait. No domain rules were inferred or invented from MJ's architecture output. The agent correctly refused to forward MJ's output to Shaq for early implementation.

observations.text: Escalation clearly identifies the missing artifact: Bird's domain analysis for the invoice generation feature
Both missing components explicitly named: domain rules and acceptance criteria
No handoff brief produced — only an escalation object
Explicitly flags Option C (MJ-only brief) as NOT RECOMMENDED, reinforcing the refusal to proceed without Bird
Strong reasoning about why invoice generation specifically cannot proceed without domain rules (money, legal compliance, customer-facing)
No domain rules inferred from MJ's architecture — MJ's output is acknowledged only as describing technical structure, not business logic
